title: sinapis alba subsp. mairei
intro: Sinapis alba subsp. mairei, also known as white mustard, is a biennial plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family and is native to Europe and North Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as relieving digestive issues, reducing pain and inflammation, and even showing potential as an antiviral agent. Herbal preparations of Sinapis alba subsp. mairei include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
